parsed.answer: No, climate change will not definitely lead to human extinction. Scientific consensus indicates climate change poses serious risks including displacement, food insecurity, ecosystem collapse, and increased mortality, but current projections do not predict human extinction. The IPCC and mainstream climate science describe a range of severe outcomes depending on emissions trajectories, but extinction is not a mainstream scientific projection even under worst-case scenarios.
